etymology.description: Depicts a plant sprout (屮) with a middle emphasis line -> emphasizes sprout
etymology.interpretation: The character 屯(tún, 'station') originally depicts a plant sprout with an added line to emphasize its growth. This imagery reflects the concept of development or establishment, leading to the sense of 'station' as a place where something is established or set up.

examples.chinese: 这附近有一个储备粮的屯(這附近有一個儲備糧的屯)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè fùjìn yǒu yīgè chǔbèi liáng de tún.
examples.english: There is a grain storage station nearby.

examples.chinese: 他们准备在此地设立一个新的军事屯(他們準備在此地設立一個新的軍事屯)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen zhǔnbèi zài cǐdì shèlì yīgè xīn de jūnshì tún.
examples.english: They plan to establish a new military station at this location.

examples.chinese: 村民们在屯里储存了大量的粮食(村民們在屯裡儲存了大量的糧食)。
examples.pinyin: Cūnmín men zài tún lǐ chǔcún le dàliàng de liángshí.
examples.english: The villagers have stored a large amount of grain in the station.
